steps.prompt: Which words form the first verb phrase?

hints.Mortuus only: 'Mortuus' is a participle that needs the auxiliary 'erat' to form a complete verb
hints.erat only: 'Erat' is the auxiliary — together with 'mortuus' it forms the pluperfect passive
hints.et revixit: 'Et revixit' is the second clause — the first verb phrase is 'mortuus erat'

steps.reveal: mortuus = having died (perf. ptcp. of deponent 'morior')

hints.present active participle: A present active participle would be 'moriens' — 'mortuus' is perfect
hints.future active participle: A future active participle would be 'moriturus' — 'mortuus' is perfect
hints.gerundive: A gerundive would be 'moriendus' — 'mortuus' is a perfect participle

steps.reveal: mortuus erat = he had died / he was dead

hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'mortuus est' — 'erat' (imperfect of 'esse') makes this pluperfect
hints.imperfect: Imperfect of 'mori' would be 'moriebatur' — 'mortuus erat' is pluperfect
hints.future perfect: Future perfect would be 'mortuus erit' — 'erat' makes this pluperfect

hints.moror, morari, moratus sum, —: These are the parts of 'morari' (to delay) — a deponent but different verb
hints.mordeo, mordere, momordi, morsum: These are the parts of 'mordere' (to bite)
hints.moveo, movere, movi, motum: These are the parts of 'movere' (to move)

steps.target: to come back to life / to revive
choices: to come back to life / to revive

hints.to return: 'To return' is 'revenire' or 'redire' — 'reviviscere' means 'to come back to life'

steps.reveal: revixit = he came back to life

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'reviviscit' — 'revixit' has the perfect stem
hints.pluperfect indicative: Pluperfect would be 'revixerat' — 'revixit' is simple perfect

hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live) — the base verb without 're-'
hints.reviso, revisere, revisi, revisum: These are the parts of 'revisere' (to revisit/see again)
hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come)

steps.prompt: What is the rhetorical contrast in this phrase?
steps.target: death vs. new life (mortuus vs. revixit)
choices: death vs. new life (mortuus vs. revixit)

hints.past vs. present tense: Both verbs are past tense — the contrast is semantic: death vs. revival
hints.active vs. passive voice: 'Mortuus erat' is deponent (active meaning) and 'revixit' is active — the contrast is death/life
hints.singular vs. plural subject: The subject is singular throughout — the contrast is between dying and coming back to life

translation: He was dead and has come back to life
